sources.title: Selected Example
sources.why_it_matters: Selected example is a VC market-framing piece (b2venture) useful for taxonomy and wedge thinking but must be supplemented with primary-source evidence and traction data.

sources.title: The Evolution of the LLM Toolstack - Thoughts on Capturing Value in the GenAI Tech Stack as a Startup
Langfuse blog and OSS project (LLM observability)
b2venture - The Evolution of the LLM Toolstack
sources.why_it_matters: Structured VC perspective that proposes a three-stage taxonomy for the LLM toolstack and highlights candidate categories and examples. Useful for shaping high-level market stages and wedge thinking.
evidence: Claim: LLM toolstack is at "best-of-breed" stage (stage 2) with fragmentation and opportunity to move to end-to-end solutions. (Support: Article argument and category listing; cites examples of vendors and categories)
open_questions: What empirical traction signals (funding, customers, adoption) support the "stage 2" claim?
Which vendors listed have measurable traction and which are marketing examples only?
draft_implications: Use the three-stage taxonomy as a candidate market map but triangulate with adoption/traction data from other sources.

sources.title: OpenAI - evals (GitHub)
sources.why_it_matters: Primary-source technical framework for evaluation; useful to model the evaluation and benchmarking tooling category, methodology, and adoption.
evidence: Claim: OpenAI offers a structured, maintained evals framework for LLM assessment. (Support: repo README, docs, examples)
open_questions: How widely adopted is OpenAI evals vs alternatives (e.g., EleutherAI harness, LM-Eval)? Need GH stars, forks, and citations.
draft_implications: Use OpenAI evals as canonical example of eval tooling; capture repo-level signals (stars, forks) for traction evidence.

sources.title: Langfuse (OSS observability)
sources.why_it_matters: Concrete open-source product covering observability, evals, prompt management for LLMs. Helps illustrate observability category and production deployment considerations.
evidence: Claim: Langfuse provides tracing, evals, prompt management, integrates with LangChain and OpenTelemetry; has blog posts about scaling and infrastructure. (Support: blog posts, GitHub repo, docs)
open_questions: Traction metrics (customers, GitHub activity, funding) vs competitors.
draft_implications: Use Langfuse as a concrete case study for LLM observability and note gaps such as standardized trace schemas and cross-vendor telemetry.
1. Expand candidate list of exemplar market-mapping reports and vendor analyses (e.g., a16z essays, Menlo/VC pieces, State of AI report) using leet_websearch.
2. Gather traction signals for central providers: OpenAI, Langfuse, Fiddler, LlamaIndex, LangChain, Weights & Biases, Humanloop, etc.
3. Retrieve GitHub metrics (stars, forks, updated_at) for major OSS projects: openai/evals, langfuse/langfuse, EleutherAI/lm-evaluation-harness, LlamaIndex, LangChain.
4. Create document manifest and outline based on a normalized selected example.

sources.title: The Evolution of the LLM Toolstack - Thoughts on Capturing Value in the GenAI Tech Stack as a Startup
sources.notes: Selected as structural exemplar for taxonomy and wedge-framing; opinionated, needs triangulation with primary sources.

sources.title: openai/evals
sources.notes: Canonical example of an eval framework; useful for methodology and eval tooling category.

sources.title: langfuse/langfuse
sources.notes: Representative LLM observability OSS project with cloud and self-host options; good case study for tracing and deployment tradeoffs.

sources.title: langchain-ai/langchain
sources.notes: De-facto agent engineering framework and integration hub; essential for developer experience and agent orchestration analysis.
